G = nx.Graph() #creates an empty network graph
G2 = nx.Graph() 
G3 = nx.Graph() 
G4 = nx.Graph()

nodes = ("Paul", "John", "Helen", "Mirriam", "Luke", "Davis") #our nodes, labeled 1,2,3,4,5,6.

edges4 = [("Paul", "John")]
edges = [("Paul", "John"), ("John", "Helen"), ("Helen", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Luke"), 
         ("Davis","Luke"), ("Mirriam", "John"), ("Mirriam", "Paul")]
edges2 = [("Paul", "John"), ("Helen", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Luke"), 
         ("Davis","Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Paul")]
edges3 = [("Paul", "John"), ("John", "Helen"), ("Helen", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Luke"), 
         ("Davis","Luke"), ("Mirriam", "John"), ("Mirriam", "Paul"), 
        ("Paul","Helen"), ("Davis", "Paul"), ("Luke", "John"), ("Helen", "Mirriam")]

G.add_nodes_from(nodes)
G.add_edges_from(edges)

G2.add_nodes_from(nodes)
G2.add_edges_from(edges2)

G3.add_nodes_from(nodes)
G3.add_edges_from(edges3)

G4.add_nodes_from(nodes)
G4.add_edges_from(edges4)


density4 = nx.density(G4)
density = nx.density(G)
density2 = nx.density(G2)
density3 = nx.density(G3)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(18, 18))
fig.set_facecolor('lightblue')

axes[0, 0].set_title(f"Density = {density}", size=30)
axes[0, 1].set_title(f"Density = {density2}", size=30)
axes[1, 0].set_title(f"Density = {density3}", size=30)
axes[1, 1].set_title(f"Density = {density4}", size=30)

plt.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.98, top=0.98, bottom=0.02)
pos = nx.random_layout(G, seed=100)

nx.draw_networkx(G, pos=pos, ax=axes[0, 0], node_size=2500, node_color="salmon", edgecolors="black", alpha=0.8)
pos2 = nx.random_layout(G2, seed=100)
nx.draw_networkx(G2, pos=pos2, ax=axes[0, 1], node_size=2500, node_color="salmon", edgecolors="black", alpha=0.8)

pos3 = nx.random_layout(G3, seed=100)
nx.draw_networkx(G3, pos=pos3, ax=axes[1, 0], node_size=2500, node_color="salmon", edgecolors="black", alpha=0.8)

pos4 = nx.random_layout(G4, seed=100)
nx.draw_networkx(G4, pos=pos4, ax=axes[1, 1], node_size=2500, node_color="salmon", edgecolors="black", alpha=0.8)

# Save the figure
plt.savefig("example_density.png")
plt.show()
import networkx as nx
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Create an empty network graph
G = nx.Graph()

# Define nodes and edges
nodes = ("Paul", "John", "Helen", "Mirriam", "Luke", "Davis")
edges = [("Paul", "John"), ("John", "Helen"), ("Helen", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Luke"), 
         ("Davis", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "John"), ("Mirriam", "Paul"), 
         ("Paul", "Helen"), ("Davis", "Paul"), ("Luke", "John"), ("Helen", "Mirriam")]

# Add nodes and edges to the graph
G.add_nodes_from(nodes)
G.add_edges_from(edges)

# Compute closeness centrality
centrality = nx.closeness_centrality(G)
centrality_values = np.array(list(centrality.values()))
centrality_standard = np.argsort(np.argsort(centrality_values))
norm = plt.Normalize(min(centrality_standard), max(centrality_standard), clip=False) 
cmap = plt.cm.YlGnBu

# Normalize centrality values for coloring
norm = plt.Normalize(vmin=min(centrality_values), vmax=max(centrality_values))
cmap = plt.cm.YlGnBu

# Compute node positions only once
pos = nx.random_layout(G, seed=100)

# Set up the plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.98, top=0.98, bottom=0.02)

# Draw nodes, edges, and labels using the same positions
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(G, pos=pos, node_size=2500, 
                       node_color=centrality_standard, cmap=cmap, edgecolors="black", alpha=1, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_edges(G, pos=pos, alpha=0.6, ax=ax)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(G, pos=pos, ax=ax)

# Create a ScalarMappable and add a color bar
sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap=cmap, norm=norm)
sm.set_array([])
plt.colorbar(sm, ax=ax)

# Save the plot
plt.savefig("example_graph_closeness_centrality.png")
plt.show()
G = nx.DiGraph() #creates an empty network graph

nodes = ("Paul", "John", "Helen", "Mirriam", "Luke", "Davis") #our nodes, labeled 1,2,3,4,5,6.
edges = [("Paul", "John"), ("John", "Helen"), ("Helen", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Luke"), 
         ("Davis","Luke"), ("Mirriam", "John"), ("Mirriam", "Paul")]
G.add_nodes_from(nodes) #the `add_nodes_from()` command adds nodes to the network
G.add_edges_from(edges)
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.98, top=0.98, bottom=0.02)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(G,  pos=nx.random_layout(G, seed=100), node_size=2500, 
                       node_color="salmon", edgecolors="black",alpha=0.6)
nx.draw_networkx_edges(G, pos=nx.random_layout(G, seed=100), alpha=1, arrows=True, arrowsize=20)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(G, pos=nx.random_layout(G, seed=100))

plt.savefig("example_graph_dir.png")
 
G = nx.Graph() #creates an empty network graph

nodes = ("Paul", "John", "Helen", "Mirriam", "Luke", "Davis") #our nodes, labeled 1,2,3,4,5,6.
edges = [("Paul", "John"), ("John", "Helen"), ("Helen", "Luke"), ("Mirriam", "Luke"), 
         ("Davis","Luke"), ("Mirriam", "John"), ("Mirriam", "Paul")]
G.add_nodes_from(nodes) #the `add_nodes_from()` command adds nodes to the network
G.add_edges_from(edges)

degree=dict(G.degree())

def get_node_colors(degree):
    if degree == 1:
        return 'blue'
    elif degree == 2:
        return 'green'
    elif degree == 3:
        return 'yellow'
    else:
        return 'red' 

color_map = [] #`color_map` is an empty list

for node in nodes:
  color = get_node_colors(degree[node]) 
  color_map.append(color)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.98, top=0.98, bottom=0.02)
nx.draw_networkx_nodes(G,  pos=nx.random_layout(G, seed=100), node_size=2500, 
                       node_color=color_map, edgecolors="black",alpha=0.8)
nx.draw_networkx_edges(G, pos=nx.random_layout(G, seed=100), alpha=0.5)
nx.draw_networkx_labels(G, pos=nx.random_layout(G, seed=100))



blue_patch = mpatches.Patch(color='blue', label='degree 1') 
green_patch = mpatches.Patch(color='green', label='degree 2')
yellow_patch = mpatches.Patch(color='yellow', label='degree 3')
plt.legend(handles=[blue_patch, green_patch, yellow_patch]) #adds legend to the plot


plt.savefig("example_graph_2.png")
degree
